title: SECTION 1 — DATASYNC MODULE TESTS

tests.test case: Auto store registration on plugin install
tests.ecom360 side: Verify SyncConnection record created in DB with store name, platform=magento2, URL

tests.steps: 1. Wait 5 minutes after setup 2. Check logs
tests.magento side: Plugin sends periodic heartbeat via cron
Magento plugin checks this on config page

tests.steps: Use wrong API key in X-Ecom360-Key header
1. Trigger bin/magento ecom360:sync:products 2. Or wait for cron
tests.verify on ecom360: Go to DataSync admin → Products. Count must match Magento

tests.steps: 1. Add new product in Magento admin 2. Wait for observer/cron
tests.verify on ecom360: New product appears in synced_products collection in MongoDB

tests.steps: 1. Update product price in Magento 2. Wait for sync
tests.verify on ecom360: Updated price reflects in Ecom360 within cron cycle

tests.steps: 1. Delete a product in Magento 2. Check Ecom360
tests.verify on ecom360: Product marked inactive or removed in synced_products

tests.steps: Use a configurable product with size/color options
tests.verify on ecom360: All variants appear with correct attributes in Ecom360

tests.steps: Check synced product has image URLs
tests.verify on magento: Product has multiple images in Magento
tests.verify on ecom360: Image URLs are present and accessible in synced_products
Both prices synced correctly, special_price field populated

tests.steps: Set product to out of stock
tests.verify on ecom360: Inventory synced as out_of_stock = true in Ecom360

tests.verify on magento: Product assigned to multiple category IDs
tests.verify on ecom360: synced_product has all category_ids array populated
All products synced, check SyncLog for created/updated/failed counts
synced_categories has full hierarchy with parent_id relationships
New category appears in Ecom360 with correct parent

Updated name reflected in Ecom360

tests.steps: Place a test order on Magento staging storefront
tests.verify on ecom360: Order appears in synced_orders with all line items, shipping, payment

tests.steps: Change order status from Processing to Complete
tests.verify on ecom360: Order status updated in synced_orders

tests.steps: Create a credit memo / refund in Magento
tests.verify on ecom360: Refund event recorded in Ecom360
Shipment tracking data appears in synced order

tests.steps: Place order as guest without login
tests.verify on ecom360: Order syncs with guest email, no customer_id reference
PII (email, name, address) is removed/masked in synced order
Orders are not synced at all when consent = none
All orders synced, SyncLog shows correct counts

tests.steps: Register new account on Magento storefront
tests.verify on ecom360: New customer appears in synced_customers with email, name
Login event recorded in tracking_events in MongoDB
Updated customer data in synced_customers
Full customer profile with email, name, address in Ecom360
Customer in Ecom360 has no identifiable PII, only behavioral data
All customers synced correctly

tests.steps: Place order that reduces stock by 1
tests.verify on ecom360: Stock quantity updated in synced_inventory

tests.steps: Update product stock qty in Magento admin
tests.verify on ecom360: New stock level in synced_inventory
Low stock alert visible in Ecom360 BI module
synced_inventory has all products with current qty

tests.steps: 1. Add items to cart 2. Leave site without purchasing 3. Wait for AbandonedCartCron
tests.verify on ecom360: Cart appears in synced_abandoned_carts with products, customer info, cart value

tests.steps: Click recovery link from Ecom360 marketing email
tests.verify on ecom360: Cart marked as recovered in Ecom360
No abandoned carts in Ecom360 when consent is none

tests.steps: Add 5 products to cart then abandon
tests.verify on ecom360: All 5 items appear in synced_abandoned_carts

tests.steps: Visit staging site, check if popup widget loads
tests.verify on magento: PopupWidget block renders in frontend layout
tests.verify on ecom360: Popup configured in Ecom360 marketing settings

tests.steps: Fill and submit the popup lead capture form
tests.verify on ecom360: Lead appears in synced_popup_captures with email, name, timestamp
Lead capture works on exit intent trigger

tests.steps: Visit page and wait for popup timer
tests.verify on ecom360: Lead capture works on timed trigger

tests.steps: Go to Ecom360 admin → DataSync → Connections
tests.expected result: Shows Magento staging store connection with status, last heartbeat, platform info
Shows all sync batches with created/updated/failed counts per entity type
Shows all entity types (products, orders, customers, etc.) with their consent levels

tests.steps: Change order sync permission from full to anonymized
tests.expected result: Permission updates and next sync uses new consent level
Lists all synced products with name, SKU, price, stock, last_synced_at
Lists all synced orders with order ID, customer, total, status, line items
Lists all synced customers with profile info (based on consent level)
Lists all products with current stock levels from Magento

tests.test case: Failed sync log shows error reason
tests.expected result: Error message explains why records failed (validation, consent, etc.)

tests.steps: Check each sync batch log entry
tests.expected result: Each entry has: entity_type, created, updated, failed, timestamp, connection_id

description: DataSync is the foundation. All other modules depend on products, orders, customers and inventory being synced correctly from Magento to Ecom360. Test this first before any other module.

title: SECTION 2 — ANALYTICS MODULE TESTS

tests.steps on magento storefront: Visit any product page on staging site
tests.verify on ecom360 backend: tracking_events collection has page_view event with URL, timestamp, session_id, tenant_id

checkout_started event with cart total, item count
purchase event with order_id, revenue, items array in MongoDB

tests.steps: Go to Ecom360 admin → Analytics → Overview
tests.expected result: KPI cards show: revenue, orders, visitors, conversion rate, AOV with trend indicators

Map or table showing visitors/revenue by country, city, device type
RFM distribution chart, customer segments (Champions, Loyal, At-Risk, Lost)
Cohort table showing week-by-week customer retention percentages
Live count of active visitors, current carts, revenue today. Updates without refresh
Average session duration, pages per session, bounce rate, top entry/exit pages

tests.steps: Use UTM parameters in URL on staging, then check analytics
tests.expected result: UTM source, medium, campaign tracked. Revenue attribution per campaign visible

tests.steps: Change date range on any analytics page
tests.expected result: Data updates for selected range (Today, 7d, 30d, 90d, custom)
Top pages by views, unique visitors, average time on page
Customer lifetime value predictions with score, predicted_revenue_12mo, segment

tests.steps: Use CLV what-if endpoint: change acquisition cost
tests.expected result: Returns recalculated CLV and ROI projections
Period-over-period revenue breakdown showing gains/losses by category

tests.steps: Ask: Why did revenue change last week?
tests.expected result: AI-generated explanation with 3+ contributing factors and supporting evidence

tests.steps: Type: Show me top 5 products last month
tests.expected result: Analytics engine returns correct product list matching the query
Autocomplete suggestions appear for common analytics questions
Visual map showing customer touchpoints from first visit to purchase
Top 3 pages where customers leave the funnel identified with drop-off %
AI product recommendation list per customer segment based on behavior

tests.steps: Browse a product multiple times on staging
tests.expected result: IntentScoringService assigns score 0-100 to the customer session

tests.steps: Set alert: notify if revenue drops 20%
tests.expected result: Alert fires and appears in alerts list when threshold breached
Alert moves to acknowledged state, history recorded

tests.steps: Set trigger: show popup when cart value > $100
tests.expected result: Trigger evaluates when customer has $100+ in cart on staging
Comparison of store KPIs vs industry averages (conversion rate, AOV, etc.)

tests.steps: Create segment: Customers who purchased 3+ times
tests.expected result: Segment created with rule engine, customer count displayed
Custom event schema saved, tracker sends event, appears in analytics

tests.steps: Browse as anonymous then log in
tests.expected result: IdentityResolutionService links anonymous session to known customer profile

tests.test case: No events in date range selected
tests.expected result: Analytics shows zeros/empty state gracefully, no server error
Analytics computes correctly with minimal data
Revenue chart shows $0 without divide-by-zero errors
Dashboard loads within 5 seconds, no timeout errors
Products with &, <, > or unicode in names display correctly
Multi-currency store orders display with correct currency symbols
Known bot user-agents do not pollute analytics data
Same event sent twice only recorded once via idempotency

description: Analytics is the core intelligence engine. Test tracking events from Magento storefront flowing into Ecom360 MongoDB, then verify all dashboards and analytics endpoints return correct data.

title: SECTION 7 — ADMIN PANEL, CORE & SECURITY TESTS

tests.expected result: Filament admin panel loads, login page shown

tests.expected defense: Input sanitized, no SQL error, no data leakage

tests.attack vector: Attempt state-changing request without CSRF token from browser

tests.expected defense: tenant_id from body ignored, server uses auth context

tests.attack vector: POST to webhook endpoint with invalid signature
Attempt 10+ failed logins in quick succession
tests.expected defense: Account temporarily locked or rate limited
No passwords, API keys, or PII in plain text in log files
